Dr A B
Cardiology Clinic
Main Street
City

1 January 2026

Dear Dr Smith,
Re: Mr John Jones aged 45

I am writing to refer Mr Jones for your assessment of his recurrent chest pain.

Mr Jones is a non-smoker and consumes alcohol occasionally within recommended limits. He presented today with central chest tightness on exertion lasting two weeks. His blood pressure was 140 over 90 and his pulse was regular.

I would appreciate your specialist review. Please contact me if further information is required.

Yours sincerely,

Dr A B

1 January 2026

Dear Dr Smith,
Re: Mr John Jones aged 45

I am writing to update you regarding Mr Jones who was discharged from our ward today.

Mr Jones was presented with central chest pain and shortness of breath on admission. Initial investigations confirmed a non-ST-elevation myocardial infarction. He was managed with dual antiplatelet therapy and underwent coronary angiography during his admission.

His condition stabilised and he was discharged in good general condition. Please contact me if further information is required.

Yours sincerely,

Dr A B
